key_insight: Adding SageMath as a verifiable tool to LLM agents substantially improves performance on research-level mathematics tasks and narrows the gap between open and closed models.
engineering_relevance: This shows that CAS-augmented agent workflows can make computational math systems more accurate, efficient, and useful for iterative research and conjecture exploration.
method: Built a ReAct-style LLM agent that reasons step by step and calls SageMath for verified computation.
Used Context7 to fetch up-to-date documentation during tool use.
Refined the RealMath benchmark with multi-step post-processing and multi-stage validation to improve problem extraction quality.
evidence_signal: Across frontier models on the RealMath benchmark, SageMath access improved results by an average of +9.7 percentage points, with gains ranging from 1.5 to 27.8 points; GPT-5.5 reached 75.2% solve rate and the lowest token usage among tool-enabled setups.
watch_item: Performance depends on tool reliability and benchmark extraction quality, so agent gains may be sensitive to implementation details and validation pipeline choices.
paper_title: Evaluating SageMath-Augmented LLM Agents for Computational and Experimental Mathematics
